data.title: Sitka Gold Adds Legal and Capital Markets Expertise to Board as Drilling Expands
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Sitka Gold Corp. ( (TSE:SIG) ) just unveiled an update.

Sitka Gold has appointed corporate and securities lawyer Caroline Hendrick as an independent director, adding capital markets and M&A expertise to its board as it advances the flagship RC Gold Project in Yukon. Hendrick, a long-time advisor to the company, received 400,000 stock options exercisable over three years, while long-serving directors Greg Dawson and Peter Maclean resigned after helping guide Sitka’s evolution into a leading junior explorer, as the company continues a major 60,000-metre drill program and an active investor outreach schedule.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:SIG) stock is a Hold with a C$0.89 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sitka Gold Corp. stock, see the TSE:SIG Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SIG Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SIG is a Neutral.

The score is held back mainly by pre-revenue financials with widening losses and deeply negative cash flows, despite a low-debt balance sheet. Technicals are mildly bearish near-term, while corporate events (expanded drilling success and sizeable financings to fund a major 2026 program) provide the key offsetting support; valuation offers limited help given negative earnings and no stated yield.

To see Spark’s full report on SIG stock, click here.

More about Sitka Gold Corp.

Sitka Gold Corp. is a Canadian mineral exploration company focused on discovering economically viable gold, silver and copper deposits. The well-funded firm is advancing its 100%-owned flagship RC Gold Project in Yukon’s Tombstone Gold Belt, while also progressing the Alpha Gold Project in Nevada and permitted projects in Arizona and Nunavut.

Average Trading Volume: 1,270,184

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: C$315.3M

data.title: HSBC Raises US$2.5bn via Perpetual Contingent Convertible Securities
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

HSBC Holdings ( (GB:HSBA) ) has provided an announcement.

HSBC Holdings has issued US$2.5 billion of perpetual subordinated contingent convertible securities in two US$1.25 billion tranches, paying 6.75% and 7.00% coupons and callable during optional redemption periods in 2031 and 2036, respectively. The bank has applied for these CoCo instruments to be admitted to the Official List and to trading on the Global Exchange Market of Euronext Dublin, bolstering its additional loss-absorbing capital base while targeting sophisticated, non‑retail investors under tight distribution and regulatory constraints in the UK and EEA.

The instruments are explicitly flagged as complex and high risk, with HSBC and underwriters imposing restrictions that effectively exclude retail clients in the UK and EEA in line with MiFID II, PRIIPs and UK PRIIPs rules. This structure reflects continuing regulatory pressure on large global banks to maintain robust capital buffers and may appeal primarily to institutional investors seeking higher-yielding bank capital securities in a low-rate, regulation‑heavy environment.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:HSBA) stock is a Hold with a £1360.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on HSBC Holdings stock, see the GB:HSBA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HSBA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HSBA is a Outperform.

The score is driven by solid fundamental profitability and a constructive earnings-call outlook (clear medium-term targets and strong 2025 performance), supported by positive price momentum. The main offsets are volatile cash flows, balance-sheet/data-quality limitations in the latest year, and identified near-term credit risk (higher ECL guidance and Hong Kong CRE), while valuation and dividend are supportive but not exceptionally cheap.

To see Spark’s full report on HSBA stock, click here.

More about HSBC Holdings

HSBC Holdings plc, headquartered in London, is the parent company of HSBC and operates as one of the world’s largest banking and financial services organisations. Serving customers from offices in 56 countries and territories, the group reported assets of US$3.233 trillion at 31 December 2025, underscoring its global systemic importance and extensive international footprint.

Average Trading Volume: 24,190,793

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: £203.2B

See more data about HSBA stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: LGL Group Schedules 2026 Annual and Investor Meetings
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

LGL Group ( (LGL) ) has provided an update.

On March 24, 2026, The LGL Group, Inc. announced that its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders is scheduled for May 12, 2026, at 8:30 a.m. ET at the Harvard Club of New York City, followed by an investor meeting at 10:00 a.m. ET at the New York Stock Exchange. Proxy materials will outline the record date, voting matters and procedures, while attendance at the investor session will be subject to capacity and security constraints.

At the May 12 investor meeting, management planned to detail a strategy to build a defense technology platform within LGL through selective investments, acquisitions and connectivity partnerships, and to highlight its partnership with Legion Capital, a defense technology investment platform. Executives framed this push into national security, defense and resilient infrastructure—particularly precision timing and frequency capabilities—as a way to capitalize on rising allied defense budgets and shifting procurement priorities, positioning LGL to identify and scale mission-relevant technologies across areas such as space, autonomy, electronic warfare and cyber resilience for long-term, shareholder-focused growth.

The most recent analyst rating on (LGL) stock is a Buy with a $7.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on LGL Group stock, see the LGL Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on LGL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, LGL is a Neutral.

The score is primarily supported by balance-sheet strength (near-zero debt) but held back by weakening revenue, uneven core profitability, and softer free-cash-flow momentum. Technicals are constructive but appear overbought, and valuation looks expensive given the current earnings profile; corporate leadership changes are a moderate positive but not yet reflected in fundamentals.

To see Spark’s full report on LGL stock, click here.

More about LGL Group

The LGL Group, Inc., listed on the NYSE American under the symbol LGL, is a holding company active in services, merchant investment and manufacturing. Its subsidiary Precise Time and Frequency, LLC produces industrial electronic instruments focused on precision time and frequency solutions from a facility in Wakefield, Massachusetts, while Lynch Capital International targets value creation through investments across diversified industries.

With engineering and design roots dating back to 1917, LGL has evolved from its origins as Lynch Glass Machinery, a glass-forming equipment maker, into a diversified owner-operator in precision engineering, manufacturing and services. The company, now headquartered in Orlando, Florida, pursues a growth strategy built on expanding both new and existing operations in markets tied to critical technologies and resilient infrastructure.

Average Trading Volume: 13,489

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: $38.72M

See more insights into LGL stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Faraday Copper Extends Near-Surface and Deep Mineralization at Copper Creek
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Faraday Copper ( (TSE:FDY) ) is now available.

Faraday Copper reported new Phase IV drill results from its Copper Creek project, highlighting multiple near-surface copper intercepts in the American Eagle area that exceed open-pit cutoff grades. The company also extended mineralization at the Keel zone to the northeast, while simultaneously collecting hydrological and geotechnical data to support future technical studies and enhance the project’s open-pit and underground development potential.

Six drill holes at American Eagle confirmed the continuity of shallow mineralization above open-pit cutoff, including intercepts such as 93.05 metres at 0.44% copper and 67.00 metres at 0.40% copper. At Keel, one deep hole intersected 551.00 metres at 0.34% copper with higher-grade zones, underscoring the potential for further underground resource expansion and strengthening Faraday’s position in the copper exploration space.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:FDY) stock is a Hold with a C$4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Faraday Copper stock, see the TSE:FDY Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FDY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FDY is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (pre-revenue, widening losses, and sustained cash burn), partially offset by a low-debt balance sheet. Technicals are the main support, with price above key moving averages and positive MACD. Valuation is constrained by negative earnings and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on FDY stock, click here.

More about Faraday Copper

Faraday Copper Corp. is a Canadian mineral exploration and development company focused on advancing its Copper Creek copper project in Arizona. The company targets both near-surface and underground copper resources with the goal of defining open-pit and underground mining potential in a prominent U.S. copper-producing jurisdiction.

Average Trading Volume: 906,460

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: C$1.04B

data.title: Christopher Mills-Linked Trust Lifts Stake in River Global PLC
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from River Global PLC Class A ( (GB:RVRG) ).

River Global PLC has reported a significant share purchase by North Atlantic Smaller Companies Investment Trust PLC, a person closely associated with director Christopher Mills, which acquired 2,375,000 B Shares at 35 pence each. The transaction increases the aggregate holdings of Mills and his associated entities to 26,964,500 A Ordinary Shares, representing 16.79% of A share voting rights, and 35,622,500 B Shares, representing 24.75% of B share voting rights.

The disclosure underscores Mills’s substantial and growing influence over River Global’s capital structure, with multiple related entities, including Harwood Capital and Oryx International Growth Fund, holding sizeable positions across both share classes. This heightened concentration of ownership may strengthen the alignment between management and a key shareholder, while drawing investor attention to governance dynamics and potential strategic moves at the company.

More about River Global PLC Class A

River Global PLC is a U.K.-listed company with A Ordinary and B share classes traded on the London Stock Exchange, attracting investment from institutional shareholders and specialist funds. Its shareholder base includes notable investment trusts and asset managers, highlighting continued interest from professional investors in the company’s equity structure.

data.title: Prosus Continues Share Buybacks With €127 Million Repurchase Tranche
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Prosus ( (NL:PRX) ) has issued an update.

Prosus has provided an update on its ongoing open-ended share repurchase programme involving ordinary shares of both Prosus and its parent Naspers, targeting free-float shareholders. Between 16 and 20 March 2026, the company bought back 2,919,471 Prosus shares at an average price of €43.5292, for a total outlay of €127.1 million.

The latest tranche underscores Prosus’s continued use of buybacks as a capital allocation tool, which can support its share price and signal confidence in the group’s valuation and long-term strategy. The programme, conducted under EU market abuse regulations, remains a notable element in how Prosus manages its capital structure and shareholder returns within the broader global tech and e-commerce sector.

More about Prosus

Prosus N.V. is a global technology group focused on building local e-commerce champions in growth markets, serving around 2 billion customers worldwide. The company holds leading positions in food delivery, online classifieds and fintech, and, through Prosus Ventures, invests in AI, social and e-commerce platforms, B2B software, logistics, health, blockchain, agriculture and other tech-driven sectors.

data.title: Roma Green Finance Calls April 2026 EGM to Seek Share Consolidation Authority
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Roma Green Finance Limited ( (ROMA) ) is now available.

Roma Green Finance Limited has called an extraordinary general meeting of shareholders to be held in Hong Kong on April 15, 2026, with March 19, 2026 set as the record date for voting eligibility. The board is soliciting proxies from holders of its Class A, Class B and undesignated shares, encouraging investors to vote even if they do not attend in person.

At the meeting, shareholders will vote on an ordinary resolution authorizing a share consolidation across all classes at a ratio between one-for-two and one-for-250, with the exact ratio and timing to be set by the board by October 12, 2026. The proposal would give directors broad discretion to implement or abandon the consolidation and to manage fractional entitlements, underscoring the company’s effort to preserve flexibility over its capital structure and potential share price normalization, a move of particular relevance to Nasdaq-listed issuers facing minimum price and liquidity considerations.

More about Roma Green Finance Limited

Roma Green Finance Limited is an exempted company incorporated in the Cayman Islands with limited liability and listed on Nasdaq under the ticker ROMA. The company is headquartered in Wanchai, Hong Kong, and has a share capital structure comprising Class A ordinary shares, Class B ordinary shares and undesignated shares, all with a par value of US$0.001 each.

Average Trading Volume: 573,522

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: $353.2M

data.title: United Laboratories Balances Profit Dip With Bigger Bet on Innovative Drugs
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from The United Laboratories International Holdings ( (HK:3933) ) is now available.

The United Laboratories reported 2025 revenue of RMB13.21 billion, down 4% year on year, with profit attributable to shareholders falling 21.6% to RMB2.09 billion and basic earnings per share declining to RMB1.1065. The board proposed a final dividend of RMB0.26 per share, taking the full-year payout to RMB0.42 and implying a reduced payout ratio of 38%, reflecting more cautious capital distribution amid weaker earnings.

Against a backdrop of intensifying Chinese policy support for innovative drugs, the group is doubling down on an innovation-led strategy that prioritises high-end R&D and globalisation. It now has 23 Class I new drug candidates under development, including UBT251 and UBT37034 injections for obesity, diabetes, MASH and chronic kidney disease, with advancing clinical programmes in China and the U.S. expected to support its long-term transformation and competitive position despite near-term profit pressure.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3933) stock is a Hold with a HK$12.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on The United Laboratories International Holdings stock, see the HK:3933 Stock Forecast page.

More about The United Laboratories International Holdings

The United Laboratories International Holdings is a Mainland China-based pharmaceutical group listed in Hong Kong, engaged in the research, development, manufacturing and sale of drugs. The company focuses on innovative Class I drug candidates and generic medicines, with a growing emphasis on obesity, diabetes, metabolic and kidney disease treatments and international market expansion.

Average Trading Volume: 11,348,779

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: HK$19.93B

Fluidra ( (ES:FDR) ) has provided an announcement.

Fluidra has disclosed a new tranche of transactions under its ongoing share buyback programme, which is designed to fund a global share purchase incentive plan for employees. The programme, previously launched and later extended, underscores the company’s continued reliance on stock-based incentives to align staff interests with shareholder value and manage its capital structure.

On 16 March 2026 the company repurchased 7,065 of its own shares on Spanish stock markets at an average price of about €20.09 per share, with trades executed across a range of prices close to that level. The modest scale and routine nature of the purchases suggest an operational execution of an existing remuneration plan rather than a shift in strategy, but they marginally reduce free float while potentially supporting employee engagement and long-term retention.
